items.title: Cadence Rakes In $100M to Automate Chronic Care at Scale
items.summary: Cadence raised $100 million in Series C funding to scale its AI-powered chronic care platform, which monitors patients at home and adjusts their medications in real time. The startup, now valued at $1.23 billion, partners with health systems to treat patients with heart failure, hypertension and diabetes. The post Cadence Rakes In $100M to Automate Chronic Care at Scale appeared first on MedCity News .

items.title: Anthropic Backer Menlo Ventures Raises $3B In New Funds To Back AI Startups Across Stages
items.summary: Venture firm Menlo Ventures has raised $3 billion across two new funds, the largest capital raise in its 50-year history, to invest in AI startups from seed through growth stage. The firm says the new capital will target companies in sectors from enterprise tools to healthcare.

items.title: b9830
items.summary: common : allow --offline in llama download ( #25091 ) Expose the existing --offline flag to llama download so a script can run it to check whether a model is already cached and ready to be served without touching the network. Also fix a latent use-after-free in the URL-task on_done callback: first_path is block-scoped and was captured by reference, but invoked after the block ends. items.title: OpenNMT/CTranslate2
items.summary: Fast inference engine for Transformer models CTranslate2 CTranslate2 is a C++ and Python library for efficient inference with Transformer models. The project implements a custom runtime that applies many performance optimization techniques such as weights quantization, layers fusion, batch reordering, etc., to accelerate and reduce the memory usage of Transformer models on CPU and GPU. The following model types are currently supported: Encoder-decoder models: Transformer base/big, M2M-100, NLLB…
